feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: I don't understand why Toby Fox created those four fucking rooms with the pink s…
entry.content: I don't understand why Toby Fox created those four fucking rooms with the pink sunset and had the secret boss say he loves sunsets, yet didn't tie it all together in any way. Those four rooms have absolutely no connection to the boss fight—even though in other parts of the castle, the sunset color changed depending on the flower.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: >In 1995, Lou Reed filmed an unaired television pilot called Dirty Boulevard, in…
entry.content: >In 1995, Lou Reed filmed an unaired television pilot called Dirty Boulevard, intended to be a New York centric series that followed him through his diners, delis, coffee shops, bars, and longtime local hot spots across the city. Despite interest in the concept, the pilot was never picked up. https://youtu.be/__MxC5vFQ5I?si=IGC FuWloJFAwrCap

entry.title: /g/ Technology: ENERGIZER BATTERIES
entry.content: woah... a desktop chat client that unifies IRC, XMPP, and Mumble???? AND it has voice, video, AND screensharing implemented? AND it was programmed in C??? AND IT DOES SHIT THAT EVERY SINGLE CLIENT DEVELOPER IS TOO MUCH OF A PUSSY TO DO FOR SOME REASON???? HORY SHET IT FUCKING WORKS!!! No need for Gajim + HexChat + Mumble, No need for the barely working pidgin + a unfinished xmpp library.. WHEN I HAVE TRICHAT! It's incredibly modular, so other toolkits can have interfaces implemented easily. I ca

entry.title: /b/ Random: I'm BORED of this heat. Why does it need to be this hot every single day? It's j…
entry.content: I'm BORED of this heat. Why does it need to be this hot every single day? It's just dumb. I'll never complain about a chilly grey day ever again. How do people in places like the Philippines deal with this all year round? Having moist armpits, moist STINKY balls, moist feet, feeling absolutely drained. It's just hell. I can't even go and see a prostitute because I'm all nasty and hour after showering

entry.title: /b/ Random: The autistic online male's perception of femininity is fascinating because it's …
entry.content: The autistic online male's perception of femininity is fascinating because it's informed almost entirely by anime and video games, due to lack of socialisation especially with the opposite sex. His innate sensitivity becomes transmuted by the only social world he feels comfortable in. He wants to become an anime girl. Doing this could actually reward him some social currency in the leftist world but he lacks the aesthetic awareness to do it well and he's too autistic to be outside socialising wi

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Linux'es huge gaming improvement
entry.content: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Pr oton/releases/tag/proton-11.0-1 Valve has released Proton 11.0-1, a major update to its Windows compatibility layer for Linux that makes more games playable while fixing a long list of bugs affecting existing titles. The release restores compatibility for many EA games after a recent EA App update, moves classics like Resident Evil (1996), Resident Evil 2 (1998), Dino Crisis, and SHOGUN: Total War from Proton Experimental into the stable release, and adds supp

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: It is impossible to visualize the scale of the universe. In the piece of the uni…
entry.content: It is impossible to visualize the scale of the universe. In the piece of the universe we can see, there are something like roughly two trillion galaxies. We haven't counted them all, but that's an estimate based on surveys of the local universe. Two trillion. Two thousand billion galaxies. And each galaxy is, let's say, around the size of the Milky Way. Some are bigger, some are smaller. But the Milky Way has 400 billion stars in it. It takes light over 100,000 years to cross a galaxy. And there

entry.title: /g/ Technology: What went wrong with Linus Tech Tips?
entry.content: Any time I was about to make a big purchase on computer tech, I used to check to see if Linus Tech Tips had a review about it before purchasing. Nowadays I don't even bother since it seems like he's been out of touch for a while. Can anyone pinpoint when it all started going downhill for LTT and their reviews? I was excited when he showcased that he had spent millions in lab testing equipment to really put tech to the test, but then decided not to really use it in any videos. What was the point

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: TRASH/ - OC - DRAWTHREAD/RP Thread
entry.content: Ice Cream Edition >RP Have your OCs interact! Coffeeshop AU, multiversal merge, whatever floats your boat >general Follow /trash/ and global rules Be cool to drawfriends Art critiques only if asked No one's owed a delivery >requesters Reply to the Request Anchor Post your OC only: one post, reference image included (pic > description) Must be your own OC (franchise-based fine, as long as it's actually yours) Repost your request each thread; don't just link the old one Not everything gets filled;

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Just a reminder
entry.content: If its proprietary, then it's compromised. >operating system --> Hardened Gentoo Linux w/ refused proprietary packages upon setup, refuse to install any proprietary packages, harden the kernel, secure the bootchain and use Sway. >storage --> LiteX and ECP NAND/NOR >FPGAs --> Lattice ECP5 >architecture --> RISC-V (CVA6 softcore on ECP5 is good) >web browser --> GNU Icecat or Firefox ESR/Hardened Firefox >GPU --> AMD Pitcairn or Tahiti >monitor --> Modos paper display w/ ECP5 controller in SBC for

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Fates Worse Than Death
entry.content: Some fates in video games that are worse than dying Stalker from Half-Life 2 (also all the headcrab-zombies)

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: The outrage for Sony isn't just about not having physical copies.
entry.content: I can buy a used copy of Silent Hill f on Ebay for less than 40 dollars. On the digital store, I have to pay 70 dollars for the standard edition. This digital-only shit is a means to make people pay more. And what's worse, is the new mechanic they're introducing, called "Dynamic Pricing." This is some EA Games level of fuckery that I never thought I'd see come out of a japanese company.

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Paint damage - car wash and key scratches
entry.content: I fucked up by putting my car through an automatic car wash, and then I fucked up again by parking it near where a bunch of brown warehouse workers take their smoke break. Now it has swirls from the car wash, and key scratches from the brown warehouse workers. What's the best way to make my car look like new? Pic related is before the paint damage.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: hating rap is a self-report that you are a manchild. being able to appreciate ra…
entry.content: hating rap is a self-report that you are a manchild. being able to appreciate rap as a white person means you successfully expanded your palate and perspective beyond what was spoonfed to you as a child. there is nothing "based" in admitting your tastes have remained the same for the past 20 years, its embarrassing actually. a person's opinion on rap is a great litmus test for how open-minded they are and their willingness see the world from a different point of view.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Tom Tom Club [Sire, 1981] Vaguely annoyed by "Wordy Rappinghood"'s arch, prolix …
entry.content: Tom Tom Club [Sire, 1981] Vaguely annoyed by "Wordy Rappinghood"'s arch, prolix postverbalism, I resisted this record until the "Genius of Love" signature, already the basis of two rap covers, caught up with me in a club in Queens. Now I enjoy every cut, even the one with the radio transmission, a device I ordinarily regard as the worst permutation of the worst fad of the year. Between Tina Weymouth's childish vocals and the consistently playful pulse, the best kiddie funk since Motor-Booty Affa

entry.title: /g/ Technology: 2x24” or 2x27” ?
/g/ Technology: Lua-related technology
entry.content: >WHY SHOULD I USE LUA? Lua is the greatest embeddable scripting language of all time! It's very fast and compatible because it's written in ANSI C, and it's small enough that it can be embedded anywhere. The language spec is very small, with just 1 of every type (1 number type, 1 data structure type, etc.) It has very advanced metaprogramming features. It can be used as a simple config language but is flexible enough to be an advanced programming language in its own right. Lua is ideal if you wa

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Everyone sleeps on the fact that's possible to build any operating system at all…
entry.content: Everyone sleeps on the fact that's possible to build any operating system at all on top of the Linux kernel. It doesn't have to use a single line of traditional GNU or Unix userspace and it doesn't need to follow POSIX semantics. The kernel can be used purely for hw support.

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: my onion on Puella Magi Madoka Magica Portable?
entry.content: Puella Magi Madoka Magica Portable is an just okay Madoka Magicka experience. The gameplay is not thing special and you can tell it had a low budget but they tried they're best. the story also follows a non-canon timeline from Homura's dream and a everyones live happily ever after ending where walpurgisnacht is defeated. it'm lalso reuses a LOT of assets from previous games butter nevertheless it is a overall good game and I give it a 6/10 Your fortune: Good Luck

Apple did this in 2001, it's 2026, you can fucking do it... who cares if people clinging to legacy stuff get upset? We don

entry.title: /news/ News: Trump says US will give Ukraine license to produce Patriot defense systems
entry.content: https://time.com/article/2026/07/08 /trump-zelensky-ukraine-patriot-mis sile-interceptors-russia-nato/ So much for Putin's puppet, eh? President Donald Trump told Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky that the U.S. would extend Ukraine a license to manufacture Patriot missile interceptors as the country continues to defend itself against Russia. “We'll give them the right to make Patriots. We'll show them how to do it,” Trump said on day two of the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey. “It's very comp

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Watermeth injection
entry.content: Why isn't this a standard feature on new cars? >Higher compression >Higher maximum boost pressure >More spark advance >Use lower grades of fuel >Lower NOx emissions >Leaner burn w/o cooking pistons or valves >All for the price of an extra hole in the washer reservoir and one (1) tiny pump, or a venturi valve if you're feeling extra cheap And it's already been done before. In the 1960's. On an Oldsmobile. We already have a similar ShitTank™ on diesels, why not have a similar tank on gasoline cars

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Why are so many people unaware of how great having a desktop PC is?
entry.content: I do not understand it. Having a proper desktop PC is amazing. The best thing about it is it frees up so much of your other devices. You don't need to buy a 110% laptop that can do everything you can just get something thin and light that doesn't even need dedicated graphics. You won't need a giant phone to try and do all any work on. It will easily last 5+ years and many more after that. You can upgrade them customize them no need to throw them away after a year or two. Get any monitors you wan

entry.content: I'm 3 hours in and there's still tutorials popping up every 10 minutes introducing a new system. How the fuck are you supposed to remember all this shit? Why are there so many fucking underlying systems in a game about bonking dinosaurs in the head in the first place?
